strengths: Waterfront positioning in Iskandar Puteri is a rare asset — limited comparable inventory in the immediate Medini/EduCity precinct
Hotel suites component (15%) provides diversified income stream and supports hospitality demand from EduCity and Legoland catchment
4.0x plot ratio on mixed-use zoning enables efficient GDV stacking across condos, retail, and hotel
Strong Singapore cross-border demand supported by Tuas Checkpoint proximity (8.5 km) and ongoing Johor-Singapore SEZ tailwinds
weaknesses: Leasehold 99-year tenure is a structural pricing discount of 10–15% vs freehold — limits premium pricing ambition
5-year development period with hotel component increases execution complexity and requires specialist operator procurement
Title search still active — encumbrance status unconfirmed; deal cannot advance to valuation phase until cleared
opportunities: Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (SEZ) designation could drive institutional demand for hotel and retail components from Singapore-registered entities
EduCity Iskandar expansion (1.2 km) creates captive demand for serviced accommodation from international students and faculty
Waterfront retail F&B (22,000 sqft) can command significant premium as destination dining — benchmarked against Danga Bay precedent
threats: Iskandar Puteri waterfront market has a track record of delayed absorption — Forest City overhang still suppresses buyer sentiment in the broader precinct
Hotel feasibility is sensitive to tourism recovery assumptions — post-pandemic RevPAR in Iskandar has been inconsistent
Currency risk: a significant proportion of target buyers are Singapore-based — SGD/MYR movements directly impact affordability

recommendation.headline: High-potential waterfront site with SEZ upside, but clear title and market absorption evidence must be confirmed before committing.
recommendation.rationale: The Iskandar Waterfront Parcel C offers genuine scarcity value in a precinct benefiting from the Singapore SEZ macro tailwind. However, the deal is at an early stage — title search is still active, valuation is pending, and the 5-year timeline with hotel complexity adds meaningful execution risk. The leasehold tenure will require careful pricing strategy to maintain buyer interest against freehold alternatives in the market.
keyConditions: Obtain clean title search confirmation with no encumbrances before releasing valuation budget — do not advance to Board Approval until cleared
Engage a hotel operator (Marriott, IHG, or Ascott) for a letter of intent before committing the 15% hotel allocation — operator backing de-risks the component materially
Stress-test GDV at 80% of blended PSF assumption to model a conservative SEZ adoption scenario before Board Paper submission
